channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: One woman's moment of connection with another patient in a psychiatric unit
item.description: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman shares a moment of connection she experienced with another patient in a psychiatric unit.
item.content:encoded: On this week's "My Unsung Hero" from Hidden Brain, one woman shares a moment of connection she experienced with another patient in a psychiatric unit.

item.title: As 'The Book of Mormon' turns 15, its original stars pop in to say 'Hello!'
item.description: Fifteen years after The Book of Mormon made its Broadway debut, original cast members Andrew Rannells and Josh Gad once again took the stage as Mormon missionaries — this time at the 2026 Tony Awards.
item.content:encoded:
Fifteen years after The Book of Mormon made its Broadway debut, original cast members Andrew Rannells and Josh Gad once again took the stage as Mormon missionaries — this time at the 2026 Tony Awards.

(Image credit: Jenny Anderson)
item.dc:creator: Terry Gross

item.title: NPR's new chief content officer: 'I've been training for this job my whole life'
item.description: Less than two weeks after overhauling its newsroom, NPR has hired Nadine Zylstra to be its chief content officer. She has been a top executive at Sesame Workshop, YouTube and Pinterest.
item.content:encoded:
Less than two weeks after overhauling its newsroom, NPR has hired Nadine Zylstra to be its chief content officer. She has been a top executive at Sesame Workshop, YouTube and Pinterest.

(Image credit: Variety via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: David Folkenflik

item.title: In his book, self-described USAID 'whistleblower' talks about the agency and Ebola
item.description: Nicholas Enrich, on staff at the U.S. Agency for International Aid under 4 administrations, talks about Into the Woodchipper: A Whistleblower's Account of How the Trump Administration Shredded USAID.
item.content:encoded:
'/>

Nicholas Enrich, on staff at the U.S. Agency for International Aid under 4 administrations, talks about Into the Woodchipper: A Whistleblower's Account of How the Trump Administration Shredded USAID.

(Image credit: Simon & Schuster)
item.dc:creator: Fatma Tanis

item.title: Recently recovered recording features voice of Harpo Marx, the 'silent' Marx brother
item.description: Harpo Marx -- the "silent" Marx brother -- can finally be heard speaking in a live album of recently recovered material, which was recorded just six months before he died in 1964.
item.content:encoded: Harpo Marx -- the "silent" Marx brother -- can finally be heard speaking in a live album of recently recovered material, which was recorded just six months before he died in 1964.
item.dc:creator: Hosts

item.title: Former Kennedy Center curator talks about the venue's future
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Tony Award winners list: 'Schmigadoon!' wins best musical, 'Death of a Salesman' lives on
item.description: The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway performances on Sunday in New York, but the star of the night was singer-songwriter P!nk, who hosted the show.
item.content:encoded:
The 79th Annual Tony Awards celebrated the best of Broadway performances on Sunday in New York, but the star of the night was singer-songwriter P!nk, who hosted the show.

(Image credit: Charles Sykes)
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Vanasco

item.title: It's cleaning season for the Art Institute of Chicago's miniature rooms
item.description: At the Art Institute of Chicago, cleaning the Thorne Rooms, shoebox-sized rooms presenting a visual history of interior design in miniature, is a meticulous and delicate process.
item.content:encoded: At the Art Institute of Chicago, cleaning the Thorne Rooms, shoebox-sized rooms presenting a visual history of interior design in miniature, is a meticulous and delicate process.
item.dc:creator: Minju Park

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: NBA Team Names
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with OPB listener Jocelyn Tutak of Portland, OR., along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe plays the puzzle with OPB listener Jocelyn Tutak of Portland, OR., along with Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: It's not just T-shirts — how Broadway merchandise is changing
item.description: Every year, we highlight essential Broadway workers not eligible for Tonys. This year, we look at the world of Broadway merch, which is a lot more than T-shirts, mugs and refrigerator magnets.
item.content:encoded:
Every year, we highlight essential Broadway workers not eligible for Tonys. This year, we look at the world of Broadway merch, which is a lot more than T-shirts, mugs and refrigerator magnets.

(Image credit: Matthew Murphy and Evan Zimmerman)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Lunden
